message:
feat(cli): add related-work relevance threshold, rename limit param, and surface feedback stats in triage output

Related work improvements (file_intel + CLI):
- Rename `--related-limit` to `--related-max-results` for clarity — the
  old name implied a hard cutoff, but the new semantics involve a
  two-stage filter: first by relevance threshold, then by result count.
- Add `--related-min-relevance` (default 20) — a minimum relevance score
  below which related beads are dropped before the max-results truncation.
  This prevents low-signal matches from polluting the output when many
  beads share incidental file overlap.
- `find_related_work()` now accepts both `min_relevance: u32` and
  `max_results: usize`, applying the relevance floor via `retain()` before
  the count truncation. All call sites and tests are updated.

Robot triage feedback stats:
- The `--robot-triage` output now includes an optional `feedback` field
  containing `FeedbackStats` (total_accepted, total_ignored counts) when
  the user has previously run `--feedback-accept` or `--feedback-ignore`.
  This lets downstream consumers (dashboards, CI) know whether the triage
  scores incorporate user feedback adjustments.
- Refactored `main.rs` to load `FeedbackData` once and derive both the
  weight adjustment map and the stats from it, avoiding a redundant disk
  read.

Benchmark hygiene:
- All three benchmark functions (`bench_triage`, `bench_plan`,
  `bench_real_fixture`) now use `..TriageOptions::default()` spread
  syntax instead of listing every field, so new fields added to
  `TriageOptions` don't cause compile errors in benchmarks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -288,8 +288,11 @@ pub struct Cli {
     #[arg(long)]
     pub robot_related: Option<String>,
 
+    #[arg(long, default_value_t = 20)]
+    pub related_min_relevance: u32,
+
     #[arg(long, default_value_t = 10)]
-    pub related_limit: usize,
+    pub related_max_results: usize,
 
     #[arg(long)]
     pub robot_blocker_chain: Option<String>,
